title: JGroups
description: JGroups transport stack configuration for Infinispan.

transport.description: Class that represents a network transport. Must implement org.infinispan.remoting.transport.Transport.

JGroupsStackDefinition.description: Defines an individual JGroups stack, either by referencing a file or inline protocol configuration.

JGroupsStackFile.description: Defines a JGroups stack by pointing to a file containing its definition.

path.description: Path of JGroups configuration file containing stack definition.

JGroupsStack.description: Defines a JGroups stack inline with protocol configurations. Protocol names are keys, their properties are values (object or null).

additionalProperties.description: A JGroups protocol. The key is the protocol name. The value is either null (use defaults) or an object with protocol properties.

JGroupsRemoteSites.description: Defines the relay configuration for cross-site replication.

default-stack.description: Defines the name of the JGroups stack to be used by default when connecting to remote sites.

cluster.description: Defines the default cluster name for remote clusters.

JGroupsRemoteSite.description: Defines a remote site for cross-site replication.

name.description: Defines the name of the remote site.

stack.description: Defines the name of the JGroups stack to use to connect to the remote site.

cluster.description: Defines the name for the underlying group communication cluster.

change_key_on_leave.description: When a node leaves, change the secret group key, preventing old members from eavesdropping

who_has_cache_timeout.description: Timeout (in ms) to determine how long to wait until a request to fetch the physical address for a given logical address will be sent again. Subsequent requests for the same physical address will therefore be spaced at least who_has_cache_timeout ms apart

after_creation_hook.description: Fully qualified name of a class implementing ProtocolHook, will be called after creation of the protocol (before init())

async_executor.enabled.description: If not enabled, tasks will executed on the runner's thread

bind_addr.description: The bind address which should be used by this transport. The following special values are also recognized: GLOBAL, SITE_LOCAL, LINK_LOCAL, NON_LOOPBACK, match-interface, match-host, match-address

bind_port.description: The port to which the transport binds. Default of 0 binds to any (ephemeral) port. See also port_range

bundler_type.description: The type of bundler used ("ring-buffer", "transfer-queue" (default), "sender-sends" or "no-bundler") or the fully qualified classname of a Bundler implementation

external_addr.description: Use "external_addr" if you have hosts on different networks, behind firewalls. local_transport_class.description: The fully qualified name of a class implementing LocalTransport

log_discard_msgs.description: whether or not warnings about messages from different groups are logged

mcast_send_buf_size.description: Send buffer size of the multicast datagram socket

message_processing_policy.description: The fully qualified name of a class implementing MessageProcessingPolicy

port_range.description: The range of valid ports: [bind_port .. bind_port+port_range ]. 0 only binds to bind_port and fails if taken

receive_on_all_interfaces.description: If true, the transport should use all available interfaces to receive multicast messages

rtt.num_reqs.description: The number of RPCs to send

rtt.oob.description: Send requests and responses as OOB messages

stats.description: Determines whether to collect statistics (and expose them via JMX). Default is true

suppress_time_different_cluster_warnings.description: Time during which identical warnings about messages from a member from a different cluster will be suppressed. 0 disables this (every warning will be logged). Setting the log level to ERROR also disables this.

suppress_time_different_version_warnings.description: Time during which identical warnings about messages from a member with a different version will be suppressed. 0 disables this (every warning will be logged). Setting the log level to ERROR also disables this.

thread_pool.thread_dumps_enabled.description: Dump threads when the thread pool is full

thread_pool.thread_pool_full_suppress_ti.description: Time (in milliseconds) during which thread-pool full messages are suppressed

time_service_interval.description: Interval (in ms) at which the time service updates its timestamp. 0 disables the time service

tos.description: Traffic class for sending unicast and multicast datagrams

ucast_receiver_threads.description: Number of unicast receiver threads, all reading from the same DatagramSocket. If de-serialization is slow, increasing the number of receiver threads might yield better performance.

delay_site_unreachable_events.description: Number of millis by which SITE_UNREACHABLE events are delayed; see RelayTest.testFailover() for details

delay_sites_down.description: When a site master goes down, delay the sitesDown() callback if we have multiple members; we know that another member will become site master (https://issues.redhat.com/browse/JGRP-2708)

ergonomics.description: Enables ergonomics: dynamically find the best values for properties at runtime

id.description: Give the protocol a different ID if needed so we can have multiple instances of it in the same stack

max_site_masters.description: Maximum number of site masters. Setting this to a value greater than 1 means that we can have multiple site masters. If the value is greater than the number of cluster nodes, everyone in the site will be a site master (and thus join the global cluster

site.description: Name of the site; must be defined in the configuration

site_master_picker_impl.description: Fully qualified name of a class implementing SiteMasterPicker

site_masters_ratio.description: Ratio of members that are site masters, out of range [0..1] (0 disables this). The number of site masters is computes as Math.min(max_site_masters, view.size() * site_masters_ratio). See https://issues.redhat.com/browse/JGRP-2581 for details

stats.description: Determines whether to collect statistics (and expose them via JMX). Default is true

suppress_time_no_route_errors.description: Time during which identical errors about no route to host will be suppressed. 0 disables this (every error will be logged).

topo.global_views.description: When true, members joining or leaving any site in the network are triggering the view handler callback
